column7: Promotes robust panicle formation; split N application reduces luxury consumption and lodging risk.
Supports early root establishment; phosphorus component promotes root branching and uptake.
Improves grain nitrogen accumulation; particularly effective in alkaline or calcareous soils.
Supports high grain weight; potassium improves phloem transport of assimilates to grains.
Enhances hybrid stress tolerance through brassinosteroid signaling; beneficial during reproductive stage to reduce spikelet sterility.
Fulvic acid improves transport of nutrients across cell membranes; enhances photosynthate production in high-yield hybrid.
Stimulates N-fixing bacteria near root zone; partially offsets mineral N requirement while improving root health.
Adds up to 30 kg N/ha organically; decomposed Azolla biomass improves soil cation exchange capacity for hybrid fields.
Rapid nutrient correction at critical stages maximizes hybrid yield; foliar K at flag leaf stage improves grain filling.
